title: Joby Aviation (JOBY) Q3 2025 Earnings Transcript
text: In its Q3 2025 earnings call, Joby Aviation reported revenue of $15.8 million for the quarter. The company incurred a net loss of $401 million reflecting continued investment in FAA certification and manufacturing expansion. Joby's test aircraft completed more than 850 flights in 2025—a 260 percent increase over 2024. The company is progressing through final FAA certification stages with expected TIA testing in 2026. Management discussed the company's progress toward commercial operations, with first passenger flights expected in late 2025 or early 2026. The company's manufacturing footprint continues to expand with Ohio facility capacity expanded to nearly 1.5 million square feet. The company was selected as a partner in multiple winning White House eIPP applications. Management reaffirmed its focus on achieving 2026 commercialization goals. The company maintains a strong cash position supporting operations through certification completion and initial commercial launch.
